hypothesis: If combining pre-training on large chemical databases with domain-specific fine-tuning and expert-guided reinforcement learning improves generative model performance for de novo CTF materials discovery, then configurations incorporating all three knowledge transfer strategies (pre-training, fine-tuning, and reinforcement learning with chemically-informed reward functions) will outperform configurations using fewer transfer learning components across standard molecular generation metrics—including validity, novelty, internal diversity, and percentage passing expert filters—because sequential knowledge transfer progressively constrains the chemical space toward structurally and functionally relevant porous carbon frameworks.
